narrative:
10 congressional district has 525,190 registered voters, with 57.4% unaffiliated or lacking primary history. Republican-leaning voters make up 23.5% of registrants and Democratic-leaning voters account for 19.2%, a modest Republican lean of 4.3 percentage points. Registration is trending bluer in younger age cohorts (1990s–2000s births lean -0.2% D−R vs -9.2% for 1950s–60s cohorts).
